g.text: STEP 1: every node knows current membership via gossip
STEP 3: C 'emerges' as leader without holding an election
SAFETY HAZARD: split-brain during gossip lag

text.tspan: Alive members (gossiped, every node has same view):
Node A (id=42)
Node B (id=17)
Node C (id=88) u2190 highest
Node D (id=53)
leader(members) = node with max(id)
u2192 every node computes: leader = C
u2192 no messages exchanged u2014 just local computation
over the gossip view
C accepts leader-only operations.
Other nodes route requests to C.
No election RPCs, no votes, no terms.
When C fails:
u2022 gossip propagates 'C is down' (within seconds)
u2022 every node re-computes: leader = max(remaining) = D (id=53)
u2022 D begins accepting leader-only operations
u2022 NO ELECTION u2014 leadership simply emerges from membership
Problem: during the few seconds between
'C fails' and 'all nodes know C failed',
some nodes still think C is leader, others think D is.
If C wakes up just then u2014 split brain!
Both C and D might process leader-only operations.
Defenses:
u2022 pair with fencing tokens (generation-clock.md)
u2022 use a 'stable' view (require N rounds of agreement)
u2022 accept eventual consistency for the operations involved
Used by: SWIM-based service discovery, some control planes,
Apache Zookeeper for some scenarios, leader-by-min-id schemes.
